title: DB2 Service Stored Procedures
text: DB2 Service Stored Procedures DB2 service stored procedures can be invoked by creating a Uniface signature and using the activate ProcScript command. To create and activate a stored procedure in a DB2 database: Create the procedure in DB2. For rules on defining and invoking a stored procedure within DB2, refer to the DB2 Application Programming Manual In Uniface, create a Stored Procedure signature to represent the stored procedure. Uniface supports Unicode in stored procedure parameters. Parameters with type GRAPHIC can be used to pass UTF16 data to and from a stored procedure. Parameters with type CHAR can be used to pass UTF8 data to and from a stored procedure as long as $DEF_CHARSET is UTF8 Ensure that the signature of the procedure uses the corresponding Uniface data type. For more information, see DB2 Data Packing Make the stored procedure signature available to the Uniface Server or application that is to run the stored procedure. Depending on the location of the compiled signature, you can copy it to the target location or UAR and move it to the target location via the deployment utility, FTP, or some other means. Add the $SSP path to your assignment file: The Server parameter is optional. $DB2 DB2: Server : DatabaseName | DB2User | DB2Password $SSP $DB2 Copy Invoke the stored procedure via the activate ProcScript statement. For example: activate "MYSCHEMA".mystoredproc("A", $2) Copy This runs the stored procedure name ‘ mystoredproc ’, residing in schema ‘ MYSCHEMA ’ and defined by signature ‘ MYSCHEMA ’, passing one input parameter ‘ A ’ and one output parameter $2 It is also possible to invoke stored procedures using the call SQL instruction. For more information, see Calling Stored Procedures from SQL Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
